full_prompt:
Design the release hinge mechanism for a single-panel 1.2 m x 0.8 m spacecraft solar array, governed by NASA-STD-5017B Design and Development Requirements for Mechanisms (2022-12-06). The hinge is a motorized (spring-driven with eddy-current damper) single-axis rotary mechanism that releases a launch-restraint pin after launch and deploys the panel from its stowed orientation to 90 degrees. NASA-STD-5017B Section 4.2.4 requires a torque margin of >= 100% over the sum of resisting torques (i.e. available torque >= 2 x resisting torque sum) at worst-case conditions, and Section 4.7 requires life-test factor >= 4x service life.

Geometry. Two titanium hinge leaves bolted to the spacecraft bus and the array backside. Hinge pin: Ti-6Al-4V 8 mm diameter x 60 mm long. Bearing: two deep-groove ball bearings SKF-style, ID 8 mm, OD 22 mm, width 7 mm, dry lubricated with MoS2. Torsion spring: 302 stainless music wire, mean coil diameter 18 mm, wire diameter 2 mm, active coils 15, designed to deliver at least 0.4 N*m torque at 90 degrees deployed and 1.0 N*m at stowed (0 degrees). Eddy-current damper: inline rotary, damping coefficient 0.05 N*m*s/rad. Launch-restraint pin: separate single-shot pyro-actuated pin puller at the array edge, treated as a boolean state change (stowed vs deployed) for this analysis.

Panel inertia. Array panel + substrate total mass 3.5 kg; moment of inertia about hinge axis J = (1/3) * m * L^2 = (1/3) * 3.5 * 1.2^2 = 1.68 kg*m^2.

Resisting torques at worst-case (cold, end-of-life, bearing contaminated). T_harness = 0.08 N*m (stiff harness bending across hinge). T_friction_bearing = 0.05 N*m (dry MoS2 bearing with end-of-life degradation factor 2x per 5017B). T_detent = 0.02 N*m at 0 deg, 0 N*m after opening. T_restraint = 0 after pin release. Sum of resisting torques at any angle: T_resist = 0.08 + 0.05 + 0.02 = 0.15 N*m (max at stowed) and 0.13 N*m after detent clears.

Deployment time budget. Must reach 90 degrees within 120 seconds and velocity at 90 degrees (latch-in) must be between 3 and 15 deg/s to avoid latch damage.

Pass/fail criteria. (R1) Torque margin per NASA-STD-5017B Section 4.2.4: at every deployment angle, (T_available - T_resist) / T_resist >= 1.0 (= 100% margin). At stowed: T_spring = 1.0 N*m, T_resist = 0.15 N*m => (1.0 - 0.15) / 0.15 = 5.67 >> 1.0. At 90 deg: T_spring = 0.4 N*m, T_resist = 0.13 N*m => (0.4 - 0.13) / 0.13 = 2.08 > 1.0. (R2) Life-test factor: the mechanism must survive 4 x expected cycles. Expected service = 1 deployment + 0 retracts (one-shot), but Section 4.7 requires at least 20 ground ambient actuations + 4 thermal-vacuum qualification actuations for verification; analysis-wise the hinge must show bearing fatigue life >= 80 deployments with no increase in resisting torque beyond 0.15 N*m. (R3) Deployment time: integrate J * theta_ddot = T_spring(theta) - T_resist - c * theta_dot; must reach 90 deg in <= 120 s. (R4) Latch-in velocity at 90 deg: 3 deg/s <= theta_dot <= 15 deg/s. (R5) Hinge pin max von Mises at latch-in impact (kinetic energy 0.5 * J * omega^2 dissipated in 5 mm pin bending) must not exceed Fty_Ti / 1.25 = 688 MPa. (R6) Bearing DN value = d_m (mean bearing ID in mm) x rpm; for this slow deployment DN is near zero, but the bearing selected must have a rated static load capacity >= 5 x launch inertia load (per 5017B) = 5 x (3.5 kg * 30 g * 9.81) = 5150 N.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary analysis: (a) closed-form torque margin calculation at 0, 30, 60, 90 deg (R1); (b) ODE integration of the rotary equation of motion for R3 and R4; (c) linear-static FEM of hinge pin under latch-in impact for R5 (TET10 mesh 0.5 mm at shear plane); (d) bearing capacity lookup from SKF catalog for R6. Structural FEM required for R5; thermal, fluid, vibration, radiation, buckling NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of hinge assembly, torque margin table, deployment-angle time history plot data (theta vs t), latch-in velocity in deg/s, hinge pin max von Mises in MPa, and bearing static capacity margin.
short_prompt:
Design the launch-restraint release hinge for a single 1.2 m x 0.8 m spacecraft solar array panel (3.5 kg total, moment of inertia about hinge axis J = 1.68 kg*m^2). The mechanism is a single-axis rotary hinge driven by a torsion spring and damped by an eddy-current damper; a separate pyro pin puller releases the launch restraint. Design to NASA-STD-5017B (2022-12-06), which requires >= 100% torque margin over resisting torques at every angle and a 4x life-test factor.

Envelope: two Ti hinge leaves, Ti-6Al-4V hinge pin ~8 mm x 60 mm, two dry-MoS2 ball bearings, 302 SS torsion spring delivering 1.0 N*m stowed and 0.4 N*m at 90 deg, eddy-current damper 0.05 N*m*s/rad.

Load cases.
- LC1 torque margin at each angle with cold / end-of-life resisting torques summing to ~0.15 N*m at stowed.
- LC2 life demonstration: analytical hinge life >= 80 deployments (4x service + qual margin).
- LC3 deployment time integration: reach 90 deg in <= 120 s with latch-in velocity in 3-15 deg/s.
- LC4 latch-in impact on the hinge pin from the residual kinetic energy at 90 deg.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Deployment kinematics require ODE integration of torque balance.

prompt.title: Solar-array launch-restraint hinge per NASA-STD-5017B
prompt.brief: Torsion-spring + eddy-damper single-axis hinge deploying 3.5 kg array in <=120 s with >=100% torque margin and bounded latch-in velocity.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: NASA-STD-5017B Section 4.7 life factor 4x (20 ground + 4 TVAC => 80 analytical)
5x launch inertia per NASA-STD-5017B static-load factor

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Launch vibration enveloped by quasi-static 30 g bearing load.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Cold-case friction increase rolled into degradation factor.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Pyro pin puller qualification per NASA-STD-5017B Section 4.8
Harness stiffness measurement at cold temperature

eval_coverage: NASA-STD-5017B 100% torque margin at every angle
